hypothesis: Multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization that couples the fast MD/GCMC simulations developed here with long-timescale MD validation will exhibit surrogate model representation sensitivity that inverts as a function of fidelity level—specifically, polymer-specific language model embeddings (PolyNC, polyBERT) will provide the dominant advantage at the low-fidelity query stage where data are scarce, but general chemical space embeddings will become comparably or more effective at the high-fidelity stage because the conformational and packing features that differentiate high-performing branched and crosslinked architectures from linear ones are not captured by monomer-level PSMILES encodings, making this crossover point in relative embedding utility a measurable and architecture-dependent threshold that can be identified by systematically varying the ratio of low-to-high fidelity queries across polymer topologies.
